channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: With this simple gadget, you can make great-tasting pour-over coffee with fewer beans
item.description: Brewing coffee is all about extraction, and the right kettle will help enormously. Here's how to choose one.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Japanese leader says he hopes Osaka expo will help reunite a divided world
item.description: Japanese Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba has attended the opening ceremony for Expo 2025 Osaka and expressed his hope that the event will help restore global unity in a world is plagued by conflicts and trade wars.
item.dc:creator: Ayaka Mcgill And Mari Yamaguchi, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Waymo robotaxis make up 20% of Uber rides in Austin, data shows
item.description: Waymo robotaxis made up about 20% of rides offered by Uber Technologies Inc. in Austin in the last week of March, underscoring how consumers have warmed to the autonomous service since the companies launched a partnership early last month.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Silicon Valley needs to get back to silicon
item.description: With their drab gray suits and their Buddy Holly glasses, the so-called traitorous eight don’t look like revolutionaries. Given no context, you can imagine them occupying some kind of middle-management role at a small regional bank. And yet these are the people you can thank for the digital world.
item.dc:creator: Rodrigo Aramburu, Fast Company

item.title: A quick win for DOGE? Make procurement competitive again
item.description: If DOGE is looking for a high-value, high-return target, the software procurement process is an easy win. The post A quick win for DOGE? Make procurement competitive again first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: OpenAI countersues Elon Musk in legal dispute over ChatGPT maker's business ambitions
item.description: OpenAI is suing Elon Musk for unfair competition and interfering with its business relationships with investors and customers, escalating a legal battle between the ChatGPT maker and the billionaire who helped bankroll the artificial intelligence startup a decade ago.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Google reaffirms $75B AI infra investment as cloud providers pursue divergent strategies
item.description: In a move closely watched by enterprise technology leaders, Alphabet CEO Sundar Pichai has reaffirmed Google’s commitment to spending $75 billion this year on AI infrastructure and data centers — weeks after Microsoft reportedly abandoned many of its data center projects.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: A Counterattack in the AI Wars: OpenAI Sues Elon Musk Over Disruptive Campaign
item.description: OpenAI countersues Elon Musk, accusing him of a sustained campaign to damage the company and seeking a US federal court injunction to prevent further actions deemed unlawful and disruptive.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: New federal data threats demand new mitigation technologies
item.description: Gina Scinta, the deputy CTO at Thales Trusted Cyber Technologies, says new survey data provides insights for how agencies better secure data and networks. The post New federal data threats demand new mitigation technologies first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: OPM digitizes federal retirement booklets as part of longtime modernization effort
item.description: OPM said the digital versions of four federal retirement booklets are immediately available for download and should reduce delays for federal retirees. The post OPM digitizes federal retirement booklets as part of longtime modernization effort first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Drew Friedman, Federal News Network

item.title: Five things to consider before you deploy an LLM
item.description: If the screwdriver were invented by the tech industry today, then it would be widely deployed for a variety of tasks, including hammering nails. Since the debut of ChatGPT, there has been growing fervor and backlash against large language models (LLMs).
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Riverbed bolsters AIOps platform with predictive and agentic AI
item.description: Riverbed this week unveiled updates to its AIOps and observability platform that the company says will transform how IT organizations manage complex distributed infrastructure and data more efficiently and tame the proliferation of monitoring tools.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Microsoft workers say they've been fired after 50th anniversary protest over Israel contract
item.description: Microsoft has fired two employees who interrupted the company's 50th anniversary celebration to protest its work supplying artificial intelligence technology to the Israeli military, according to a group representing the workers.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Five tips for incoming CIOs under the new administration
item.description: Max Everett, a former Energy Department CIO, offers some advice for how new agency technology leaders can deal with some basic challenges. The post Five tips for incoming CIOs under the new administration first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Amanda Nguyen to become first Vietnamese American woman in space
item.description: Nguyen will join Aisha Bowe, Gayle King, Katy Perry, Kerianne Flynn and Lauren Sánchez on an all-female flight.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation
